summary: The commenter urges ONC to reconsider removing the Consolidated CDA creation certification criterion, emphasizing that consolidated documents provide a clearer, more accessible summary of patient visits than segmented FHIR resources. They highlight the importance of these documents for patients and caregivers to understand and share health information effectively.
key_arguments: FHIR resources are segmented and not easily or quickly navigable for patients or caregivers seeking a clear summary of events.
Consolidated CDA documents provide a complete and timely picture of clinical events, such as ER visits.
Consolidated documents facilitate easy sharing of health information with others.
Valuable patient and caregiver information exists only in consolidated documents and would be lost if removed.
recommendations: Reconsider and retain the Consolidated CDA creation certification criterion in §170.315(g)(6).
notable_quote: The loss of this significant and valuable document will have a negative impact on patients and caregivers.
